text: Should Philadelphia be allowed to pass its own taxes to pay for SEPTA?
description: Right now, Philly must ask the state for transit money. This would let the city raise its own money to keep buses and trains running.
whyItMatters: Without a new way to pay for it, SEPTA might have to cut many bus lines. This would make it harder for you to get to work or school.

text: Should the government only give money to Philly if the city builds big apartment buildings near train stations?
description: This is about 'density.' Some say more apartments near trains make rent cheaper. Others want to keep neighborhoods the way they are.
whyItMatters: This decides if your neighborhood gets more big apartment buildings or stays mostly houses.

text: Should the government give more money to small neighborhood clinics instead of big insurance companies?
description: This would put more doctors and health vans directly in Philly neighborhoods that don't have enough clinics.
whyItMatters: It changes how easy it is for you to walk to a doctor instead of taking a long bus ride to a big hospital.

text: Should the lowest pay in Pennsylvania be raised to $15 an hour right now?
description: PA's minimum wage has been $7.25 for years. Raising it would double the pay for the city's lowest earners.
whyItMatters: This directly affects your paycheck if you work in retail, fast food, or home care.

text: Should voters be allowed to rank their candidates (1st choice, 2nd choice) instead of picking just one?
description: This is 'Ranked-Choice Voting.' It lets you support your favorite person without 'wasting' your vote.
whyItMatters: This could help 'outsider' candidates win against powerful political machines in Philly.

text: Should the government close or completely change the agency that handles immigration (ICE)?
description: Some candidates want to end ICE because of how it treats people. Others think it is needed for safety.
whyItMatters: This affects many families in Philly who have relatives from other countries.

text: Should the government erase student loan debt for people who make less than $125,000 a year?
description: This would cancel the money people owe for college, giving them more money for rent or groceries.
whyItMatters: If you or your kids have college debt, this could save you hundreds of dollars a month.

text: Should the government provide one health insurance plan (Medicare) for every person in the country?
description: This is 'Medicare for All.' It would replace private insurance with a government plan that has no bills.
whyItMatters: It would change how you pay for medicine and see a dentist or doctor.

text: Should the state give families tax money to help pay for private or religious schools?
description: This uses public money for private tuition. Some say it gives kids choices; others say it takes money from public schools.
whyItMatters: This could change how much funding your local Philly neighborhood school receives.
